title: Embedding and index tradeoffs
description: Dense embeddings capture semantics but miss exact terms; sparse/keyword matches exact tokens; index types trade recall for speed.
body: Use when choosing how to represent and search the corpus. Dense embeddings retrieve by semantic similarity but can miss rare exact terms (names, codes); sparse/keyword retrieval matches exact tokens but misses paraphrase; hybrid combines both. Index types differ too: flat is exact but slow at scale, HNSW and IVF approximate for speed at some recall cost. Do not assume dense alone covers exact-match queries, and avoid an approximate index without measuring its recall. Actions: pick dense, sparse, or hybrid by query type and tune the index to the latency/recall budget. Validation signals include a recall comparison across configurations on a labeled set. Risk boundary: an approximate index silently lowering recall starves grounding; measure recall, do not assume it.
